steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank forms an incomplete independent clause ending with they found something never before seen in the fossil [blank]. The right side contains text that should begin a new independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the colon-and-period rules
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration, and a period separates independent clauses.

Option D correctly applies both: the colon after record introduces evidence of cloning, and the period separates this complete thought from the next independent clause starting with In clonal fragmentation.

The other options either misplace punctuation marks, create ambiguous connections, or fail to establish proper sentence boundaries.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
